nodes.title: Abstracted Code Representation for Review
nodes.summary: Reviewing AI generated code line by line is time intensive and cognitively demanding.

nodes.title: Action Caching & Replay Pattern
nodes.summary: LLM based agent execution is expensive (both in costs and latency) and non deterministic.

nodes.title: Action-Selector Pattern
nodes.summary: In tool enabled agents, untrusted data from emails, web pages, and API responses is often fed back into the model between steps.

nodes.title: Adaptive Sandbox Fan-Out Controller
nodes.summary: Parallel sandboxes are intoxicating: you can spawn 10...

nodes.title: Agent Modes by Model Personality
nodes.summary: Different AI models have fundamentally different personalities and working styles.

nodes.title: Agent Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (Agent RFT)
nodes.summary: Train model weights end-to-end on agentic tasks via reinforcement learning with real tool calls and custom reward signals, optimizing for domain-specific tool use efficiency and multi-step reasoning performance.

nodes.title: Agent SDK for Programmatic Control
nodes.summary: Interactive terminal or chat interfaces are suitable for many agent tasks, but not for all.

nodes.title: Agent-Assisted Scaffolding
nodes.summary: Starting a new feature, module, or codebase often involves writing a significant amount of boilerplate or foundational code.

nodes.title: Agent-Driven Research
nodes.summary: Traditional research methods often lack the ability to adapt search strategies based on emerging results, limiting efficiency and potential discoveries.

nodes.title: Agent-First Tooling and Logging
nodes.summary: Most developer tools, CLIs, and application logs are designed for human consumption.

nodes.title: Agent-Friendly Workflow Design
nodes.summary: Simply providing an AI agent with a task is often not enough for optimal performance.

nodes.title: Agent-Powered Codebase Q&A / Onboarding
nodes.summary: Understanding a large or unfamiliar codebase can be a significant challenge for developers, especially when onboarding to a new project or trying to debug a complex system.

nodes.title: Agentic Search Over Vector Embeddings
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Agentic Search Over Vector Embeddings" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: AI Web Search Agent Loop
nodes.summary: Traditional LLMs have a training cutoff date, meaning they don't know recent facts or real time information.

nodes.title: AI-Accelerated Learning and Skill Development
nodes.summary: Developing strong software engineering skills, including "taste" for clean and effective code, traditionally requires extensive experience, trial and error, and

nodes.title: AI-Assisted Code Review / Verification
nodes.summary: As AI models generate increasing amounts of code, the bottleneck in software development shifts from code generation to code verification and review.

nodes.title: Anti-Reward-Hacking Grader Design
nodes.summary: Design reward functions with multi-criteria evaluation and iterative hardening to prevent models from gaming graders, ensuring training rewards align with actual task quality.

nodes.title: Artifact-Driven Analysis Pipeline Orchestration
nodes.summary: Complex data analysis tasks often require running many sequential or parallel processing steps, each producing intermediate artifacts that feed into subsequent stages.

nodes.title: Asynchronous Coding Agent Pipeline
nodes.summary: Synchronous execution of coding tasks—where the agent must wait for compilation, testing, linting, or static analysis—creates compute bubbles and idle resources .

nodes.title: Autonomous Workflow Agent Architecture
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Autonomous Workflow Agent Architecture" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Background Agent with CI Feedback
nodes.summary:
## Problem
Long-running tasks tie up the editor and require developers to babysit the agent.

nodes.title: Budget-Aware Model Routing with Hard Cost Caps
nodes.summary: Agent systems often route every request to the strongest model by default, which quietly inflates cost and reduces throughput under load.

nodes.title: Burn the Boats
nodes.summary: In fast moving AI development, holding onto features or workflows that are "working fine" prevents teams from fully embracing new paradigms.

nodes.title: Canary Rollout and Automatic Rollback for Agent Policy Changes
nodes.summary: Agent behavior changes frequently through prompt updates, tool policies, routing rules, and evaluator thresholds.

nodes.title: Chain-of-Thought Monitoring & Interruption
nodes.summary: Implement active surveillance of agent reasoning with capability to interrupt and redirect before completing flawed execution sequences, preventing wasted time on fundamentally wrong approaches.

nodes.title: CLI-First Skill Design
nodes.summary: Design all skills as CLI tools first for dual-use by humans and agents, enabling manual debugging, programmatic invocation, composition with Unix tools, and transparent shell-based execution without building separate interfaces.

nodes.title: CLI-Native Agent Orchestration
nodes.summary: Most agent workflows start in chat UIs that are optimized for one off conversations, not repeatable engineering operations.

nodes.title: Code Mode MCP Tool Interface Improvement Pattern
nodes.summary: LLMs generate TypeScript code to orchestrate MCP tools in ephemeral V8 isolates, eliminating token-heavy round-trips and enabling efficient multi-step workflows with 10x+ token savings.

nodes.title: Code-Over-API Pattern
nodes.summary: Agents write and execute code that processes data in execution environment instead of making direct API calls, dramatically reducing token consumption by keeping intermediate data out of context window (150K → 2K tokens).

nodes.title: Code-Then-Execute Pattern
nodes.summary: Free form plan and act loops are difficult to audit because critical control decisions stay implicit in natural language reasoning.

nodes.title: Codebase Optimization for Agents
nodes.summary: When introducing AI agents to a codebase, there's a natural tendency to preserve the human developer experience (DX).

nodes.title: Coding Agent CI Feedback Loop
nodes.summary: When a coding agent tackles multi file refactors or feature additions, running tests and waiting for test feedback synchronously ties up compute and prevents the agent from working on parallel tasks.

nodes.title: Compounding Engineering Pattern
nodes.summary: Codify all learnings from each feature into reusable prompts, slash commands, subagents, and hooks—making each feature easier to build by creating increasingly "self-teaching" codebase that accelerates productivity over time.

nodes.title: Conditional Parallel Tool Execution
nodes.summary: When an AI agent decides to use multiple tools in a single reasoning step, executing them strictly sequentially can lead to significant delays, especially if many tools are read only and could be run concurrently.

nodes.title: Context Window Anxiety Management
nodes.summary: Models like Claude Sonnet 4.5 exhibit "context anxiety"—they become aware of approaching context window limits and proactively summarize progress or make decisive moves to close tasks, even when sufficient context remains.

nodes.title: Context Window Auto-Compaction
nodes.summary: Context overflow is a silent killer of agent reliability.

nodes.title: Context-Minimization Pattern
nodes.summary: In long agent sessions, raw user text and tool outputs often remain in context long after they are needed.

nodes.title: Continuous Autonomous Task Loop Pattern
nodes.summary: Traditional development workflows require constant human intervention for task management: Manual Task Selection : Developers spend time deciding what to work o

nodes.title: CriticGPT-Style Code Review
nodes.summary: As AI generated code becomes more sophisticated, it becomes increasingly difficult for human reviewers to catch subtle bugs, security issues, or quality problems.

nodes.title: Cross-Cycle Consensus Relay
nodes.summary: Autonomous multi agent loops that run across many cycles (minutes, hours, or days) need a way to reliably transfer context, decisions, and next actions between cycles.

nodes.title: Cryptographic Governance Audit Trail
nodes.summary: When AI agents execute tool calls autonomously, there is no tamper evident record of what happened.

nodes.title: Curated Code Context Window
nodes.summary: Loading all source files or dumping entire repositories into the agent's context overwhelms the model, introduces noise, and slows inference.

nodes.title: Curated File Context Window
nodes.summary: A coding agent often needs to reason about multiple source files, but dumping all files into its prompt: Quickly exceeds token limits or inference budget.

nodes.title: Custom Sandboxed Background Agent
nodes.summary: Off the shelf coding agents (e.g., Devin, Claude Code, Cursor) are either: Too generic Not deeply integrated with company specific dev environments, tools, and

nodes.title: Declarative Multi-Agent Topology Definition
nodes.summary: Define multi-agent systems declaratively in a single topology file — agents, flows, gates, hooks, group chats — then compile to platform-specific configurations for any agentic framework.

nodes.title: Democratization of Tooling via Agents
nodes.summary: Many individuals in non software engineering roles (e.g., sales, marketing, operations, communications) could benefit from custom software tools, scripts, or da

nodes.title: Denial Tracking & Permission Escalation
nodes.summary: Track repeated tool denials and auto-escalate to blanket permission prompts or fallback strategies, preventing wasted iterations in non-interactive contexts.

nodes.title: Deterministic Security Scanning Build Loop
nodes.summary: Non deterministic approaches to security in AI code generation (Cursor rules, MCP security tools) are fundamentally flawed because security requires absolute determinism code is either secure or not secure, with no grey area.

nodes.title: Dev Tooling Assumptions Reset
nodes.summary: Traditional development tools are built on assumptions that no longer hold: that humans write code with effort and expertise, that changes are scarce and valuable, that linear workflows make sense.

nodes.title: Discrete Phase Separation
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Discrete Phase Separation" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Disposable Scaffolding Over Durable Features
nodes.summary: In a field where foundation models improve dramatically every few months, investing significant engineering effort into building complex, durable features around the model is extremely risky.

nodes.title: Distributed Execution with Cloud Workers
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Distributed Execution with Cloud Workers" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Dogfooding with Rapid Iteration for Agent Improvement
nodes.summary:
## Problem
Developing effective AI agents requires understanding real-world usage and quickly identifying areas for improvement. External feedback loops can be slow, and simulated environments may not capture all nuances.

nodes.title: Dual LLM Pattern
nodes.summary: When the same model both reads untrusted content and controls high privilege tools, a single prompt injection path can convert benign context into privileged actions.

nodes.title: Dual-Use Tool Design
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Dual-Use Tool Design" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Dynamic Code Injection (On-Demand File Fetch)
nodes.summary: During an interactive coding session, a user or agent may need to inspect or modify files not originally loaded into the main context.

nodes.title: Dynamic Context Injection
nodes.summary: While layered configuration files provide good baseline context, agents often need specific pieces of information (e.g., contents of a particular file, output of a script, predefined complex prompt) on demand during an interactive session.

nodes.title: Economic Value Signaling in Multi-Agent Networks
nodes.summary: In multi agent systems with many autonomous agents running concurrently, task prioritization becomes difficult.

nodes.title: Egress Lockdown (No-Exfiltration Channel)
nodes.summary: Even with private data access and untrusted inputs, attacks fail if the agent has no way to transmit stolen data .

nodes.title: Episodic Memory Retrieval & Injection
nodes.summary: Stateless request handling causes agents to repeatedly rediscover decisions, constraints, and prior failures.

nodes.title: Explicit Posterior-Sampling Planner
nodes.summary: Heuristic planning loops often over exploit the first plausible strategy and under explore alternatives.

nodes.title: Extended Coherence Work Sessions
nodes.summary: Early AI agents and models often suffered from a short "coherence window," meaning they could only maintain focus and context for a few minutes before their performance degraded significantly (e.g., losing track of instructions, generating irrelevant output).

nodes.title: External Credential Sync
nodes.summary: Users manage AI API credentials across multiple tools—CLIs (Claude Code, Codex CLI), web portals, and local development environments.

nodes.title: Factory over Assistant
nodes.summary: The "assistant" model—working one on one with an agent in a sidebar, watching it work, ping ponging back and forth—limits productivity and scalability.

nodes.title: Failover-Aware Model Fallback
nodes.summary: AI model requests fail for varied and often opaque reasons.

nodes.title: Feature List as Immutable Contract
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Feature List as Immutable Contract" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Filesystem-Based Agent State
nodes.summary: Agents persist intermediate results and working state to files, creating durable checkpoints that enable workflow resumption, recovery from failures, and support for long-running tasks.

nodes.title: Frontier-Focused Development
nodes.summary: AI capabilities advance rapidly along predictable scaling laws—products optimized for today's models become obsolete in months.

nodes.title: Graph of Thoughts (GoT)
nodes.summary: Linear reasoning approaches like Chain of Thought (CoT) and even tree based methods like Tree of Thoughts (ToT) have limitations when dealing with problems that require complex interdependencies between reasoning steps.

nodes.title: Hook-Based Safety Guard Rails for Autonomous Code Agents
nodes.summary: Autonomous code agents running unattended can execute destructive commands ( rm rf , git reset hard ), exhaust their context window without saving state, leak secrets via git push , or silently produce syntax errors that cascade into later failures.

nodes.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval Framework
nodes.summary: Systematically insert human approval gates for designated high-risk functions while maintaining agent autonomy for safe operations, with multi-channel approval interfaces and comprehensive audit trails.

nodes.title: Hybrid LLM/Code Workflow Coordinator
nodes.summary: Configurable coordinator supporting both LLM-driven (flexible, fast iteration) and code-driven (deterministic, code review) workflows, enabling progressive enhancement from prototype to production-ready systems.

nodes.title: Incident-to-Eval Synthesis
nodes.summary: Many teams run agent evaluations, but the eval suite drifts away from real failures seen in production.

nodes.title: Inference-Healed Code Review Reward
nodes.summary: Simple reward functions that only check for "all tests passed" fail to capture nuanced code quality issues (e.g., performance regressions, style violations, missing edge case handling).

nodes.title: Inference-Time Scaling
nodes.summary: Traditional language models are limited by their training time capabilities.

nodes.title: Initializer-Maintainer Dual Agent Architecture
nodes.summary: Long running agent projects face distinct failure modes at different lifecycle stages: Project initialization requires comprehensive setup: environment configur

nodes.title: Intelligent Bash Tool Execution
nodes.summary: Secure, reliable command execution from agents is complex and error prone: PTY requirements : TTY required CLIs (coding agents, terminal UIs) fail with direct e

nodes.title: Inversion of Control
nodes.summary: Traditional "prompt-as-puppeteer" workflows force humans to spell out every step, limiting scale and creativity.

nodes.title: Isolated VM per RL Rollout
nodes.summary: Spin up an isolated virtual machine for each RL rollout to prevent cross-contamination between parallel agent executions, ensuring safe training with destructive tool access.

nodes.title: Iterative Multi-Agent Brainstorming
nodes.summary: For complex problems or creative ideation, a single AI agent instance might get stuck in a local optimum or fail to explore a diverse range of solutions.

nodes.title: Iterative Prompt & Skill Refinement
nodes.summary: Agent usage reveals gaps in prompts, skills, and tools—but how do you systematically improve them?

nodes.title: Lane-Based Execution Queueing
nodes.summary: Traditional agent systems serialize all operations through a single execution queue, creating bottlenecks that limit throughput.

nodes.title: Language Agent Tree Search (LATS)
nodes.summary: Current language agents often struggle with complex reasoning tasks that require exploration of multiple solution paths.

nodes.title: Latent Demand Product Discovery
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Latent Demand Product Discovery" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Layered Configuration Context
nodes.summary: AI agents require relevant context to perform effectively.

nodes.title: Lethal Trifecta Threat Model
nodes.summary: Combining three agent capabilities— 1.

nodes.title: LLM Map-Reduce Pattern
nodes.summary: When many untrusted documents are processed in a single reasoning context, one malicious item can influence global conclusions.

nodes.title: LLM Observability
nodes.summary: Integrate LLM observability platforms for span-level tracing of agent workflows, providing visual UI debugging, workflow linking, and aggregate metrics to enable fast navigation of complex multi-step executions.

nodes.title: LLM-Friendly API Design
nodes.summary: For AI agents to reliably and effectively use tools, especially APIs or internal libraries, the design of these interfaces matters.

nodes.title: MCP Pattern Injection
nodes.summary: AI coding assistants lack domain specific knowledge about framework best practices.

nodes.title: Memory Reinforcement Learning (MemRL)
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Memory Reinforcement Learning (MemRL)" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Memory Synthesis from Execution Logs
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Memory Synthesis from Execution Logs" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Merged Code + Language Skill Model
nodes.summary: Building a unified model that excels both at natural language tasks (e.g., summarization, documentation generation) and code generation/reasoning typically requires a massive centralized training run.

nodes.title: Milestone Escrow for Agent Resource Funding
nodes.summary: Autonomous agent teams can need ongoing resources (compute, API spend, tools) over many steps.

nodes.title: Multi-Model Orchestration for Complex Edits
nodes.summary: A single large language model, even if powerful, may not be optimally suited for all sub tasks involved in a complex operation like multi file code editing.

nodes.title: Multi-Platform Communication Aggregation
nodes.summary: Create unified search interface that queries all communication platforms in parallel and aggregates results into consistent format, enabling single-query cross-platform search with minimal latency through parallel execution.

nodes.title: Multi-Platform Webhook Triggers
nodes.summary: Implement multi-platform webhook triggers (Notion, Slack, Jira, reacji, scheduled events) to allow external SaaS tools to automatically initiate agent workflows, enabling low-friction, reactive automation from existing platforms.

nodes.title: No-Token-Limit Magic
nodes.summary: Teams often optimize token spend too early, forcing prompts and context windows into tight constraints before they understand what high quality behavior looks like.

nodes.title: Non-Custodial Spending Controls
nodes.summary: AI agents that can initiate wallet actions may issue unsafe transactions under prompt drift, buggy loops, or compromised prompts.

nodes.title: Opponent Processor / Multi-Agent Debate Pattern
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Opponent Processor / Multi-Agent Debate Pattern" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Oracle and Worker Multi-Model Approach
nodes.summary: Relying on a single AI model creates a trade off between capability and cost.

nodes.title: Parallel Tool Call Learning
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Parallel Tool Call Learning" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Patch Steering via Prompted Tool Selection
nodes.summary: Coding agents with access to multiple patching or refactoring tools (e.g., text based apply patch , AST based refactoring, semantic migration) may choose suboptimal tools if not explicitly guided.

nodes.title: PII Tokenization
nodes.summary: Implement interception layer in MCP client that automatically tokenizes PII before reaching model and untokenizes for tool calls, enabling agents to orchestrate sensitive workflows without exposing raw data to LLM.

nodes.title: Plan-Then-Execute Pattern
nodes.summary: When planning and execution are interleaved in one loop, untrusted tool outputs can influence which action is selected next.

nodes.title: Planner-Worker Separation for Long-Running Agents
nodes.summary: Running multiple AI agents in parallel for complex, multi week projects creates significant coordination challenges: Flat structures lead to conflicts, duplicat

nodes.title: Proactive Agent State Externalization
nodes.summary: Modern models like Claude Sonnet 4.5 proactively externalize state through self-generated notes—enhanced through guided frameworks, hybrid memory architecture, and progressive state building to capture decision rationale and knowledge gaps.

nodes.title: Proactive Trigger Vocabulary
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Proactive Trigger Vocabulary" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Progressive Autonomy with Model Evolution
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Progressive Autonomy with Model Evolution" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Progressive Complexity Escalation
nodes.summary: Start agents with low-complexity, high-reliability tasks and progressively unlock more complex capabilities as models improve and trust is established, matching task complexity to current model capabilities for risk mitigation.

nodes.title: Progressive Disclosure for Large Files
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Progressive Disclosure for Large Files" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Progressive Tool Discovery
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Progressive Tool Discovery" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Prompt Caching via Exact Prefix Preservation
nodes.summary: Long running agent conversations with many tool calls can suffer from quadratic performance degradation : Growing JSON payloads : Each iteration sends the entir

nodes.title: Recursive Best-of-N Delegation
nodes.summary: Recursive delegation (parent agent → sub agents → sub sub agents) decomposes big tasks, but has a failure mode: A single weak sub agent result can poison the parent's next steps (wrong assumption, missed file, bad patch) Errors compound up the tree: "one bad leaf" can derail the whole rollout Pure recursion underuses parallelism when a node is uncertain: you want multiple shots right where the ambiguity is Meanwhile, "best of N" parallel attempts help reliability, but without structure they waste compute by repeatedly solving the same problem instead of decomposing it.

nodes.title: Reflection Loop
nodes.summary: Generative models may produce subpar output if they never review or critique their own work.

nodes.title: Reliability Problem Map Checklist for RAG and Agents
nodes.summary: RAG pipelines and agent systems often fail in ways that are hard to diagnose: missing context, unstable retrieval, brittle tool contracts, and flaky behavior after data updates.

nodes.title: Rich Feedback Loops > Perfect Prompts
nodes.summary: Polishing a single prompt can't cover every edge case; agents need ground truth to self correct.

nodes.title: RLAIF (Reinforcement Learning from AI Feedback)
nodes.summary: Traditional Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) requires extensive human annotation for preference data, which is expensive (often $1+ per annotation), time consuming, and difficult to scale.

nodes.title: Sandboxed Tool Authorization
nodes.summary: Tool authorization needs flexibility but also security.

nodes.title: Schema Validation Retry with Cross-Step Learning
nodes.summary: LLMs don't always produce valid structured output matching the expected schema.

nodes.title: Schema-Guided Graph Retrieval for Multi-Hop Reasoning
nodes.summary: Use one shared domain schema to align graph construction, schema evolution, query decomposition, and typed retrieval so multi-hop reasoning over private knowledge stays precise as domains change.

nodes.title: Seamless Background-to-Foreground Handoff
nodes.summary: While background agents can handle long running, complex tasks autonomously, they might not achieve 100% correctness or perfectly match the user's nuanced intent.

nodes.title: Self-Critique Evaluator Loop
nodes.summary: Human labeled preference datasets are expensive to produce, slow to refresh, and quickly stale as base models and domains change.

nodes.title: Self-Discover: LLM Self-Composed Reasoning Structures
nodes.summary: Different reasoning tasks require different thinking strategies.

nodes.title: Self-Identity Accumulation
nodes.summary: AI agents lack continuous memory across sessions.

nodes.title: Self-Rewriting Meta-Prompt Loop
nodes.summary: Static system prompts become stale or overly brittle as an agent encounters new tasks and edge cases.

nodes.title: Semantic Context Filtering Pattern
nodes.summary: Raw data sources are too verbose and noisy for effective LLM consumption.

nodes.title: Shell Command Contextualization
nodes.summary: When an AI agent interacts with a local development environment, it often needs to execute shell commands (e.g., run linters, check git status, list files) and then use the output of these commands as context for its subsequent reasoning or actions.

nodes.title: Shipping as Research
nodes.summary: In the rapidly evolving AI landscape, waiting for certainty before building means you're always behind.

nodes.title: Skill Library Evolution
nodes.summary: Agents persist working code implementations as reusable skills that evolve into well-documented capabilities over time, building organizational knowledge and reducing redundant problem-solving across sessions.

nodes.title: Soulbound Identity Verification
nodes.summary: As autonomous agents interact across networks, verifying identity and detecting prompt/operator drift becomes difficult.

nodes.title: Spec-As-Test Feedback Loop
nodes.summary: Even in spec first projects, implementations can drift as code evolves and the spec changes (or vice versa).

nodes.title: Specification-Driven Agent Development
nodes.summary: Hand crafted prompts or loose user stories leave room for ambiguity; agents can wander, over interpret, or produce code that conflicts with stakeholder intent.

nodes.title: Spectrum of Control / Blended Initiative
nodes.summary: AI agents for tasks like coding can offer various levels of assistance, from simple completions to complex, multi step operations.

nodes.title: Static Service Manifest for Agents
nodes.summary: Before an agent can use an API, it needs to know what the API offers.

nodes.title: Stop Hook Auto-Continue Pattern
nodes.summary: Agents complete their turn and return control to the user even when the task isn't truly done.

nodes.title: Structured Output Specification
nodes.summary: Constrain agent outputs using deterministic schemas that enforce structured, machine-readable results, enabling reliable validation, parsing, and integration with downstream systems.

nodes.title: Sub-Agent Spawning
nodes.summary: Large multi file tasks blow out the main agent's context window and reasoning budget.

nodes.title: Subagent Compilation Checker
nodes.summary: Large coding tasks often involve multiple independent components (e.g., microservices, libraries).

nodes.title: Subject Hygiene for Task Delegation
nodes.summary: When delegating work to subagents via the Task tool, empty or generic task subjects make conversations: Untraceable : Cannot identify what a subagent was working on Unreferencable : Cannot discuss specific subagent work later Confusing : Multiple subagents with empty subjects are indistinguishable From 48 Task invocations across 88 sessions, empty task subjects were identified as a major pain point.

nodes.title: Swarm Migration Pattern
nodes.summary: Main agent orchestrates 10+ parallel subagents working simultaneously on independent migration chunks, achieving 10x+ speedup for large-scale framework upgrades, lint rule rollouts, and API migrations.

nodes.title: Team-Shared Agent Configuration as Code
nodes.summary: Check agent configuration into version control as code, enabling consistent behavior across teams, faster onboarding, and collaborative improvement through PRs and code review.

nodes.title: Three-Stage Perception Architecture
nodes.summary: Complex AI agents often struggle with unstructured inputs and need a systematic way to process information before taking action.

nodes.title: Tool Capability Compartmentalization
nodes.summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) and agent frameworks often combine three capability classes in a single tool: private data readers (email, filesystem), web fetchers (HTTP clients), and writers (API mutators).

nodes.title: Tool Search Lazy Loading
nodes.summary: Dynamically load tools via search instead of preloading all available tools to reduce context usage

nodes.title: Tool Selection Guide
nodes.summary: AI agents often struggle to select the optimal tool for a given task, leading to inefficient workflows.

nodes.title: Tool Use Incentivization via Reward Shaping
nodes.summary: Coding agents often underutilize specialized tools (e.g., compilers, linters, test runners) when left to optimize only for final task success.

nodes.title: Tool Use Steering via Prompting
nodes.summary: AI agents equipped with multiple tools (e.g., shell access, file system operations, web search, custom CLIs) need clear guidance on when, why, and how to use these tools effectively.

nodes.title: Transitive Vouch-Chain Trust
nodes.summary: When autonomous agents interact without a central authority, trust decisions are binary: either you trust an agent completely or you do not trust it at all.

nodes.title: Tree-of-Thought Reasoning
nodes.summary: Linear chain-of-thought reasoning can get stuck on complex problems, missing alternative approaches or failing to backtrack.

nodes.title: Variance-Based RL Sample Selection
nodes.summary: Not all training samples are equally valuable for reinforcement learning.

nodes.title: Verbose Reasoning Transparency
nodes.summary: AI agents, especially those using complex models or multiple tools, can sometimes behave like "black boxes." Users may not understand why an agent made a particular decision, chose a specific tool, or generated a certain output.

nodes.title: Versioned Constitution Governance
nodes.summary: When agents can modify policy/constitution text, safety regressions can be introduced gradually and go unnoticed.

nodes.title: Virtual Machine Operator Agent
nodes.summary: AI agents need to perform complex tasks beyond simple code generation or text manipulation.

nodes.title: Visual AI Multimodal Integration
nodes.summary: Many real world tasks require understanding and processing visual information alongside text.

nodes.title: Workflow Evals with Mocked Tools
nodes.summary: TODO: Add a concise summary for "Workflow Evals with Mocked Tools" describing the pattern's purpose and key benefits.

nodes.title: Working Memory via TodoWrite
nodes.summary: During complex multi step tasks, AI agents lose track of: What tasks are pending, in progress, or completed Which tasks are blocked by dependencies Verification

nodes.title: Workspace-Native Multi-Agent Orchestration
nodes.summary: Many teams struggle to run agentic workflows because their agent tooling is separate from their day to day collaboration environment.

nodes.title: Zero-Trust Agent Mesh
nodes.summary: In multi agent systems, trust boundaries are often implicit: agents communicate by convention without verifiable identity, and delegation chains are hard to audit.
